macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: April Jobs Report Shows Hiring Slowdown Amid Iran War Impact
items.summary: The U.S. added 115,000 jobs in April, down from 178,000 in March but exceeding economist expectations. Rising fuel prices from the ongoing Iran conflict are pressuring consumer spending, with gas prices up 50% since the war began.

items.title: Federal Reserve Shows Historic Division with Most Dissents Since 1992
items.summary: The Fed held rates steady at its April meeting but revealed rare internal dissent, with some members pushing for rate cuts as economic pressures mount. Fed Governor Stephen Miran discussed the policy divide and incoming Fed Chair Kevin Warsh's approach.

items.title: Economic Calendar Shows Mixed Global Data
items.summary: Global economic indicators for May 9 include U.S. ADP employment data, Treasury refunding announcements, and various international metrics including Brazil's PMI, Mexico's investment figures, and India's money supply data.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Crypto Market Sees $216M in Liquidations with Longs and Shorts Nearly Balanced
items.summary: The cryptocurrency market experienced $216 million in liquidations over the past 24 hours, with longs at $103 million and shorts at $113 million; Bitcoin had $28.7M long and $15.8M short liquidations, Ethereum $27.8M long and $15.7M short, affecting 80,217 users.

items.title: Bitcoin Trades at $79,926 Down 1.36% in Past 24 Hours
items.summary: Bitcoin fluctuated between $79,181 and $81,180 over the last 24 hours, currently at $79,926 as of 09:30 AM UTC, marking a 1.36% decline.

items.title: Bitcoin Faces Key Resistance at $85,200-$93,000 After 2% Dip to $79,742
items.summary: Bitcoin is trading around $79,742 after a 2% drop in 24 hours, approaching a critical resistance zone between $85,200 and $93,000; failure to break through could lead to a drop below $60,000.

items.title: VanEck Discusses Clarity Act's Potential to Boost Crypto Adoption Amid Bitcoin Dip
items.summary: Bitcoin has lost nearly half its value from its $126,000 peak in October 2025 and trades below its 200-day moving average; experts discuss how the Clarity Act could accelerate institutional crypto adoption, with strong April ETF inflows.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Learning AI Crucial for Employment as 42% of Recent Grads Underemployed
items.summary: Clara Shih, founder of New Work Foundation and former Meta executive, emphasizes that AI skills are increasingly essential for hiring amid high underemployment among young workers. She advocates for AI to benefit everyone, including the millions of unemployed 25-year-olds.

items.title: IBM CEO Study: 64% of CEOs Comfortable with AI-Driven Strategic Decisions
items.summary: IBM Institute for Business Value's annual CEO study reveals 64% of CEOs now trust AI-generated input for major decisions, marking a trust threshold in AI adoption. Discussion also covers IBM's new AI operating model and rising VC funding in AI.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: New VR Games Released This Week
items.summary: Multiple VR titles launched this week including Flatout 4: Total Insanity VR for PCVR, Spymaster for Meta Quest, and Evil Inside VR for PSVR 2, with upcoming releases like Hot Dogs, Horseshoes & Hand Grenades 2 announced.

items.title: Path of Exile 2 Patch 0.5 and Official Launch News
items.summary: Path of Exile 2 receives its biggest patch yet with version 0.5, featuring new classes including Spirit Walker and Martial Artist Monk, along with Atlas changes and a new main questline.

items.title: Escape from Tarkov Updates: Icebreaker Release and Console Expansion
items.summary: Escape from Tarkov announces Icebreaker map coming later this month, squad size reductions, and plans for both mobile and console versions of the game, plus FSR 4.0 support coming within the year.

items.title: Digimon Game Translation and Monster Taming Updates
items.summary: A Digimon game receives translation after 10 years, while Temtem raises over $500,000 USD and other monster taming titles like Lumina Tale arrive on eShop.

items.title: GTA Online Mansions Update Released
items.summary: GTA Online receives a new Mansions DLC update with new money-making opportunities and content for solo players.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Apple AI lawsuit settled: Every iPhone included in the $250 million settlement
items.summary: Apple has settled a lawsuit over Siri for $250 million, with eligible iPhone owners potentially receiving payouts.

items.title: Intel stock hits an all-time high after Apple chip deal report
items.summary: Intel's stock reached a record high following reports of a preliminary deal to produce chips for Apple.

items.title: Intel has reportedly signed a preliminary deal to produce chips for Apple
items.summary: Intel has reportedly agreed to a preliminary deal to manufacture chips for Apple, boosting its stock to an all-time high.

items.title: Nintendo is raising Switch 2 prices as chip crisis bites
items.summary: Nintendo is increasing prices for the Switch 2 amid a chip shortage, despite selling 19.86 million units last year, and forecasts lower sales ahead.

items.title: Cyberattack shutters Canvas learning platform for schools across the U.S.
items.summary: A cyberattack has disrupted the Canvas education platform, affecting schools nationwide.

items.title: Schools across U.S. disrupted after Canvas education platform hacked
items.summary: The parent company of Canvas reported a cybersecurity incident, impacting students across the U.S.

items.title: Google Health is back as Fitbit app rebrands for new tracker
items.summary: Google has rebranded its Fitbit app as Google Health and launched the screenless Fitbit Air fitness tracker.

items.title: Samsung Galaxy Buds 3 Pro receive Stability Update
items.summary: Samsung has rolled out a stability update for the Galaxy Buds 3 Pro.

items.title: Verizon lays off more employees as CEO Schulman hands out more pink slips
items.summary: Verizon continues layoffs under CEO Schulman, affecting more employees.

items.title: New Linux 'Dirty Frag' zero-day gives root on all major distros
items.summary: A new zero-day vulnerability called 'Dirty Frag' allows root access on major Linux distributions.
